documentation.text:
@dev Validates all 14 CFR / schema constraints before anchoring.
      Emits RecordRejected and reverts on any failure.
      Constraints checked:
        [1] mechanicLicense  — non-empty, >= MIN_LICENSE_LENGTH chars
        [2] rawCid           — non-empty, >= MIN_CID_LENGTH chars (non-null CID)
        [3] processedCid     — non-empty, >= MIN_CID_LENGTH chars
        [4] rawCid uniqueness — not already registered (unique File ID mapping)
        [5] timestamp        — block.timestamp > 0 and not a future spoof
        [6] tailNumber       — non-empty, >= MIN_TAIL_LENGTH
        [7] initialPrice     — must be > 0

documentation.text:
@notice Returns the fileId that registered a given CID (0 = not yet registered).
         Frontend can call this before submission to give early feedback.

devdoc.details: Implements the "automated gatekeeper" described in the paper:         Submissions are programmatically rejected if they fail to satisfy:           1. Valid 14 CFR Part 65 mechanic license reference (non-empty, min length)           2. Non-null cryptographic Content Identifier (rawCid, processedCid)           3. Unique File ID mapping (no duplicate rawCid on-chain)           4. Timestamp verification (block.timestamp must be > 0, future timestamps rejected)

params._processedCid: IPFS CID of the processed valuation CSV
params._rawCid: IPFS CID of the original maintenance log CSV

devdoc.title: AircraftMaintenanceHistory v3

addRecord(string,string,string,string,st.notice: Anchor a new FAA maintenance record on-chain.         The gatekeeper will reject submissions that fail schema constraints.
